dedup_hash is computed as SHA256 of lowercase(title) + lowercase(company) with whitespace normalized.
If a new job has the same dedup_hash as an existing job (different URL, same title+company), the function logs a warning about potential cross-source duplicate but still inserts it.
The old JSON-file-based functions (load_seen_hashes, save_seen_hashes, filter_new_only) are removed.

title: Create fast filter Pass 1 scoring prompt and orchestration
description: Create the Pass 1 fast filter scoring system. This includes: the fast_filter.md subagent prompt template that receives a batch of ~40 jobs and returns yes/no + confidence for each, and the Python orchestration code that queries unscored jobs (and stale-scored jobs) from the DB, formats them for the subagent, and writes Pass 1 results to score_dimensions. Jobs below threshold get pass=1 overall=0. Jobs above threshold get pass=1 with their confidence score, making them eligible for Pass 2. When the unscored pool exceeds 40, jobs are split into batches of 40 and 2-3 subagents run in parallel.
acceptance_criteria: pipeline/prompts/fast_filter.md exists with a structured prompt that receives a batch of job descriptions and profile data, and returns a JSON array of objects with job_id, decision (yes/no), confidence (0-100), and brief reasoning.
pipeline/src/scorer.py exists with a get_unscored_jobs(db_connection) function that returns jobs with no score_dimensions row.

pipeline/src/scorer.py has a get_stale_scored_jobs(db_connection) function that returns jobs whose score_dimensions.profile_hash differs from the current profile_hash (computed from profile.yaml + latest profile_snapshot). These jobs are re-queued for Pass 1.
pipeline/src/scorer.py has a write_pass1_results(db_connection, results) function that inserts rows into score_dimensions with pass=1.
Jobs marked 'no' in Pass 1 get overall=0 in score_dimensions.
Jobs marked 'yes' in Pass 1 get overall set to the confidence score.
The UNIQUE(job_id, pass) constraint is respected; re-running Pass 1 for the same job upserts the existing row.
When the combined unscored + stale pool exceeds 40 jobs, split_into_batches() divides them into batches of 40. Up to 3 batches can be sent to parallel subagents.
Running pytest pipeline/tests/test_scorer.py passes with mocked DB data (no actual LLM calls).
notes: All files are NEW.
GAP RESOLUTION (stale re-scoring): get_stale_scored_jobs() detects profile_hash mismatch and includes those jobs in the Pass 1 pool alongside truly unscored jobs.
GAP RESOLUTION (overflow batching): When pool > 40, split into 40-job batches and run 2-3 subagents in parallel for faster throughput.
PITFALL: Write a sentinel row before submission to mark in-flight jobs, clean up on retry.

title: Fix DB_PATH computation in web/lib/db.ts to anchor to __dirname and respect DB_PATH env var
description:
## Root Cause

`web/lib/db.ts:14` computes DB_PATH using `path.resolve(process.cwd(), "..", "data", "jobs.db")`. This anchors the path to the Node.js process's working directory at import time, not to the file's own location. When `npm run dev` is started from the repo root or from CI (rather than `cd web && npm run dev`), `process.cwd()` resolves to the wrong directory and `better-sqlite3` silently creates a new empty database one level above the repo root instead of connecting to `data/jobs.db`. Additionally, `pipeline/config/settings.py:133` reads DB_PATH from the environment (with `data/jobs.db` as fallback), but `web/lib/db.ts` ignores `process.env.DB_PATH` entirely — any developer-set DB_PATH override has no effect on the web layer. `next.config.ts:8` already uses `path.resolve(__dirname)` for a similar reason.

## Affected Surfaces

- `web/lib/db.ts:14` — `path.resolve(process.cwd(), "..", "data", "jobs.db")` (from edge-cases + drift reviews)
- `pipeline/config/settings.py:133` — reference implementation using DB_PATH env var (from drift review)

## Fix

Replace the hardcoded path constant with:
```typescript
const DB_PATH = process.env.DB_PATH ?? path.resolve(__dirname, "../../data/jobs.db");
```
This navigates from `web/lib/` up two levels to the project root regardless of the CWD at startup, and respects any DB_PATH env var set in the environment.

## Changes Needed

- `web/lib/db.ts:14`: Replace `path.resolve(process.cwd(), "..", "data", "jobs.db")` with `process.env.DB_PATH ?? path.resolve(__dirname, "../../data/jobs.db")`
- `web/next.config.ts`: Verify DB_PATH is accessible as a server-side env var (no NEXT_PUBLIC_ prefix needed for server-only use)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Starting `npm run dev` from the repo root connects to `data/jobs.db`, not `../data/jobs.db`
- [ ] Setting DB_PATH to a custom path in the environment causes `web/lib/db.ts` to use that path
- [ ] `next.config.ts` still resolves its own paths correctly (no regression)

title: Add busy_timeout = 5000 pragma to web/lib/db.ts connection to prevent SQLITE_BUSY errors
description:
## Root Cause

`web/lib/db.ts:34` sets `journal_mode = WAL` but never sets `busy_timeout`. SQLite's default busy_timeout is 0 ms — any lock contention immediately returns SQLITE_BUSY rather than retrying. `pipeline/src/database.py:221` sets `PRAGMA busy_timeout = 5000` on every connection, and database.py's module docstring (lines 6–7) explicitly states this is required for "concurrent readers from the Next.js UI." During WAL checkpoints (which briefly take an exclusive lock), web-side queries will immediately fail with SQLITE_BUSY rather than waiting up to 5 seconds for the pipeline write to finish.

## Affected Surfaces

- `web/lib/db.ts:34` — WAL pragma set, busy_timeout absent (from edge-cases + drift reviews)
- `pipeline/src/database.py:221` — Python side correctly sets busy_timeout = 5000 on every connection

## Fix

Add `db.pragma("busy_timeout = 5000")` immediately after the WAL pragma in `getDb()`.

## Changes Needed

- `web/lib/db.ts:34-35`: Add `db.pragma("busy_timeout = 5000")` after `db.pragma("journal_mode = WAL")`

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `web/lib/db.ts` calls `pragma("busy_timeout = 5000")` on every new connection
- [ ] Under concurrent pipeline writes, the web connection waits up to 5 seconds before failing (matches Python behavior)
- [ ] Existing WAL pragma behavior unchanged

title: Rename Job.raw to Job.raw_json to match DB column name and TypeScript type
description:
## Root Cause

The Python `Job` dataclass uses the field name `raw` (a V1 holdover, typed `Optional[dict]`) at `pipeline/src/models.py:73`, while the SQLite `jobs` table column is `raw_json` (TEXT, storing JSON-serialized data — `pipeline/src/database.py:72`). The TypeScript interface already uses `raw_json: string | null` (`web/lib/types.ts:27`). Any code that iterates `dataclasses.asdict(job)` and passes the result directly to an INSERT statement — as the not-yet-written deduplicator (seek-8) and fetcher adapters (seek-4, seek-5) will do — will produce a "column not found" error or silently write to the wrong column, with no dict→JSON serialization step.

## Affected Surfaces

- `pipeline/src/models.py:73` — `raw: Optional[dict]` (stale V1 name; from drift review)
- `pipeline/src/database.py:72` — `raw_json TEXT` (authoritative DB column name)
- `web/lib/types.ts:27` — `raw_json: string | null` (already correct)

## Fix

Rename the Python field to `raw_json: Optional[str]` (storing serialized JSON text, matching the DB column type). Add a `@property raw` that returns `json.loads(self.raw_json)` for any callers that need a dict. Update test fixtures that construct `Job(raw=...)`.

## Changes Needed

- `pipeline/src/models.py:73`: Rename `raw: Optional[dict]` → `raw_json: Optional[str]`
- `pipeline/src/models.py`: Add `@property raw` returning `json.loads(self.raw_json) if self.raw_json else None`
- Any test fixture using `Job(raw=...)`: update to `raw_json=json.dumps(...)`

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `Job.raw_json` field exists and is typed `Optional[str]`
- [ ] `dataclasses.asdict(job)` produces a dict with key `raw_json`, not `raw`
- [ ] A `raw` property exists for backward compatibility and returns a dict or None
- [ ] All existing tests pass after the rename

title: Fix Job.description to Optional[str] to match nullable DB column and TypeScript type
description:
## Root Cause

The three layers of the stack disagree on whether `Job.description` can be NULL. The SQLite schema at `pipeline/src/database.py:65` defines `description TEXT` with no NOT NULL constraint. The TypeScript interface at `web/lib/types.ts:20` correctly mirrors this as `description: string | null`. But the Python dataclass at `pipeline/src/models.py:42` declares `description: str` — a required, non-nullable field with no default. A job row with NULL description — valid at the DB and TypeScript levels — cannot be used to construct a Python `Job` object (Python receives `None` where `str` is expected, causing a type mismatch at runtime). When seek-4 and seek-5 fetch jobs from external APIs, some listings will have no description field.

## Affected Surfaces

- `pipeline/src/models.py:42` — `description: str` (required, non-null; from drift review)
- `pipeline/src/database.py:65` — `description TEXT` (no NOT NULL constraint; referenced by drift)
- `web/lib/types.ts:20` — `description: string | null` (already correct; referenced by drift)

## Fix

Change `pipeline/src/models.py:42` from `description: str` to `description: Optional[str] = None`. Update test fixtures that pass `description` as a required positional argument.

## Changes Needed

- `pipeline/src/models.py:42`: `description: str` → `description: Optional[str] = None`
- `pipeline/tests/test_models.py`: Update any Job fixture that passes description positionally to use a keyword argument (no functional change needed, just signature update)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `Job(title="x", company="y", url="z", source="s", location="l")` constructs successfully without description
- [ ] `Job.description` can be `None`, matching the DB schema and TypeScript type
- [ ] All existing tests pass after the change
- [ ] DB schema and TypeScript type require no changes (already nullable)

title: Unify test import strategy with pyproject.toml pythonpath to fix cross-directory ModuleNotFoundError
description:
## Root Cause

Three test files in the same directory (`pipeline/tests/`) use two incompatible import resolution strategies. `pipeline/tests/conftest.py:8` adds `pipeline/` to `sys.path` to support `from config.settings import ...` style imports (used by `test_settings.py:19`). But `pipeline/tests/test_database.py:21` uses `from pipeline.src.database import ...` and `pipeline/tests/test_models.py:19` uses `from pipeline.src.models import ...` — both require the project root (`jseeker/`) in `sys.path`, not `pipeline/`. This works when pytest is invoked as `python -m pytest` from the project root (which adds `jseeker/` automatically), but fails with `ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'pipeline'` if pytest is run from a non-standard CWD, from within a CI container with different defaults, or with `--import-mode=importlib`. `test_settings.py` passes in both cases, masking the issue.

## Affected Surfaces

- `pipeline/tests/conftest.py:8` — adds `pipeline/` to sys.path (from drift review)
- `pipeline/tests/test_database.py:21` — `from pipeline.src.database import ...` requires project root (from drift review)
- `pipeline/tests/test_models.py:19` — `from pipeline.src.models import ...` requires project root (from drift review)
- `pipeline/tests/test_settings.py:19` — `from config.settings import ...` uses conftest approach (reference)

## Fix

Add a `pyproject.toml` at the project root with `[tool.pytest.ini_options] pythonpath = ["."]`. This makes the project root reliably available in sys.path regardless of CWD or import mode, unifying both import strategies without changing any test file.

## Changes Needed

- Add `pyproject.toml` at project root:
  ```toml
  [tool.pytest.ini_options]
  pythonpath = ["."]
  ```
- Optionally remove the manual `sys.path.insert` from `conftest.py:8` if `pythonpath = [".", "pipeline"]` covers both needs.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `pytest pipeline/tests/` run from the repo root passes all tests
- [ ] `cd pipeline && pytest tests/` also passes all tests (or fails with a clear error about the working directory, not a ModuleNotFoundError)
- [ ] `test_database.py`, `test_models.py`, and `test_settings.py` all import their modules without error in both invocation styles

title: Fix normalizer.py: update all Job() calls from raw= to raw_json=json.dumps() after seek-28 rename
description:
## Root Cause

seek-28 renamed the `raw` dataclass field to `raw_json` and added a `@property raw` for backward-compatible reading. However, `pipeline/src/normalizer.py` was not updated alongside `models.py`. The `@dataclass`-generated `__init__` only accepts actual field names — `@property` decorators are not `__init__` parameters. All three normalize functions still pass `raw=<dict>` as a keyword argument to `Job()`:

- `normalize_mock` at line 21: `return Job(..., raw=raw)`
- `normalize_adzuna` at line 52: `return Job(..., raw=raw)`
- `normalize_remoteok` at line 87: `return Job(..., raw=raw)`

This raises `TypeError: Job.__init__() got an unexpected keyword argument 'raw'` on every call. All three normalize functions are completely broken — they fail immediately on invocation. This is the core data transformation path for all currently implemented pipeline sources (mock, Adzuna, RemoteOK). No job data can be ingested until this is fixed.

Additionally, the value `raw=<dict>` would need to become `raw_json=json.dumps(<dict>)` because the field type is `Optional[str]`, not `Optional[dict]`.

## Affected Surfaces

- `pipeline/src/normalizer.py:21` — `Job(..., raw=raw)` in `normalize_mock()` (from correctness R2 review)
- `pipeline/src/normalizer.py:52` — `Job(..., raw=raw)` in `normalize_adzuna()` (from correctness R2 review)
- `pipeline/src/normalizer.py:87` — `Job(..., raw=raw)` in `normalize_remoteok()` (from correctness R2 review)

## Fix

In all three Job() constructor calls in normalizer.py, change `raw=raw` to `raw_json=json.dumps(raw)`.

## Changes Needed

- `pipeline/src/normalizer.py:21`: `raw=raw` → `raw_json=json.dumps(raw)`
- `pipeline/src/normalizer.py:52`: `raw=raw` → `raw_json=json.dumps(raw)`
- `pipeline/src/normalizer.py:87`: `raw=raw` → `raw_json=json.dumps(raw)`

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `normalize_mock(...)`, `normalize_adzuna(...)`, and `normalize_remoteok(...)` all return a `Job` instance without raising `TypeError`
- [ ] The returned `Job.raw_json` is a JSON string, not a dict
- [ ] `job.raw` property on the returned `Job` returns the original dict
- [ ] All pipeline tests that call a normalize function pass

title: Guard job.raw property against JSONDecodeError on malformed raw_json
description:
## Root Cause

The `raw` backward-compat property introduced in seek-28 at `pipeline/src/models.py:79` calls `json.loads(self.raw_json)` with no exception handling:

```python
return json.loads(self.raw_json) if self.raw_json else None
```

`raw_json` is typed `Optional[str]` with no format enforcement — any string value can be stored. If the field holds invalid JSON (e.g., a truncated API response stored by an error-recovery path, a value written directly to the DB by an external tool, or partial data from a failed serialization), `json.JSONDecodeError` is raised with no application-level context. Any caller reading `job.raw` on such a row receives an unhandled exception rather than `None` or a sentinel. This will manifest once the pipeline begins writing to the DB and reading rows back.

## Affected Surfaces

- `pipeline/src/models.py:79` — `return json.loads(self.raw_json) if self.raw_json else None` (from edge-cases R2 review)

## Fix

Wrap the `json.loads()` call in a try/except:

```python
@property
def raw(self) -> Optional[dict]:
    """Backward-compatible dict view of raw_json; returns None when raw_json is None or invalid."""
    if not self.raw_json:
        return None
    try:
        return json.loads(self.raw_json)
    except json.JSONDecodeError:
        return None
```

## Changes Needed

- `pipeline/src/models.py:76-79`: Replace property body with try/except version above

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `job.raw` returns `None` when `raw_json` is `None` or empty string
- [ ] `job.raw` returns `None` (does not raise) when `raw_json` is malformed JSON
- [ ] `job.raw` returns the correct dict when `raw_json` is valid JSON

title: Fix DB_PATH empty-string edge case: use || instead of ?? in web/lib/db.ts
description:
## Root Cause

`web/lib/db.ts:15` uses nullish coalescing (`??`) to fall back to the default DB path:

```typescript
const DB_PATH = process.env.DB_PATH ?? path.resolve(__dirname, "../../data/jobs.db");
```

`??` only short-circuits on `null` or `undefined` — not on empty string. If `DB_PATH` is explicitly set to `""` in the environment (e.g., `DB_PATH= npm run dev` in a shell), `new Database("")` is called. SQLite treats an empty string path as a temporary database — no error is thrown at connection time — but all queries fail silently with "no such table" because the schema is absent. The failure mode is not obvious at the connection site.

## Affected Surfaces

- `web/lib/db.ts:15` — `process.env.DB_PATH ?? path.resolve(...)` (from edge-cases R2 review)

## Fix

Replace `??` with `||` (falsy check, treats empty string as missing):

```typescript
const DB_PATH = process.env.DB_PATH || path.resolve(__dirname, "../../data/jobs.db");
```

## Changes Needed

- `web/lib/db.ts:15`: `??` → `||`

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `DB_PATH=""` in environment falls back to the `__dirname`-anchored default path
- [ ] `DB_PATH="/custom/path"` still uses the custom path
- [ ] Unset `DB_PATH` still uses the default path

title: Fix normalizer.py: add missing source_type argument to all three Job() constructor calls
description:
## Root Cause

`source_type: str` was added as a required field on the `Job` dataclass in the seek-2 V2 model changes (`351ce4c`). `pipeline/src/normalizer.py` is a V1 port that was never updated for V2 required fields. All three normalize functions construct `Job(...)` without passing `source_type`:

- `normalize_mock` (line 16): `Job(..., raw_json=json.dumps(raw))` — no `source_type`
- `normalize_adzuna` (line 44): `Job(..., raw_json=json.dumps(raw))` — no `source_type`
- `normalize_remoteok` (line 79): `Job(..., raw_json=json.dumps(raw))` — no `source_type`

Every invocation of any normalize function raises:
```
TypeError: Job.__init__() missing 1 required positional argument: 'source_type'
```

The pipeline cannot normalize any job data until this is fixed. This is a pre-existing V1→V2 migration gap, not introduced by any recent fix commit. seek-4 does not cover normalizer.py (its scope is fetcher files only).

Found independently by both the Correctness and Edge Cases reviewers in Round 3.

## Affected Surfaces

- `pipeline/src/normalizer.py:16` — `normalize_mock()` Job() call missing `source_type` (from correctness R3 + edge-cases R3)
- `pipeline/src/normalizer.py:44` — `normalize_adzuna()` Job() call missing `source_type` (from correctness R3 + edge-cases R3)
- `pipeline/src/normalizer.py:79` — `normalize_remoteok()` Job() call missing `source_type` (from correctness R3 + edge-cases R3)

## Fix

Add `source_type=` to each Job() constructor call:

```python
# normalize_mock (line ~16):
return Job(..., raw_json=json.dumps(raw), source_type="mock")

# normalize_adzuna (line ~44):
return Job(..., raw_json=json.dumps(raw), source_type="api")

# normalize_remoteok (line ~79):
return Job(..., raw_json=json.dumps(raw), source_type="api")
```

## Changes Needed

- `pipeline/src/normalizer.py:16`: add `source_type="mock"` to Job() call in normalize_mock()
- `pipeline/src/normalizer.py:44`: add `source_type="api"` to Job() call in normalize_adzuna()
- `pipeline/src/normalizer.py:79`: add `source_type="api"` to Job() call in normalize_remoteok()

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] normalize_mock(...) returns a Job instance with source_type="mock" without raising
- [ ] normalize_adzuna(...) returns a Job instance with source_type="api" without raising
- [ ] normalize_remoteok(...) returns a Job instance with source_type="api" without raising
- [ ] All pipeline tests pass

title: V1 normalizers missing required source_type argument
description:

`pipeline/src/normalizer.py` create `Job()` instances without the required `source_type`
positional argument. The `Job` dataclass (models.py:44) requires `source_type: str` with
no default value. The seek-34 fix was documented as merged into seek-4 but the code

career_page) all correctly pass `source_type`.

## Affected Surfaces
- `pipeline/src/normalizer.py:14-24` — normalize_mock missing source_type (from correctness review)
- `pipeline/src/normalizer.py:27-55` — normalize_adzuna missing source_type (from correctness review)
- `pipeline/src/normalizer.py:58-90` — normalize_remoteok missing source_type (from correctness review)

## Fix
Add the `source_type` argument to each Job() constructor call:
- normalize_mock: add `source_type="mock"`
- normalize_adzuna: add `source_type="api"`
- normalize_remoteok: add `source_type="api"`

## Changes Needed
- `pipeline/src/normalizer.py`: Add source_type to Job() calls in normalize_mock, normalize_adzuna, normalize_remoteok

## Acceptance Criteria

title: Scrape strategy key mismatch between prompt and fetcher
description:
## Root Cause
The `company_discovery.md` prompt instructs the LLM to output scrape_strategy JSON with
keys `job_list_selector`, `job_title_selector`, `job_url_selector`, `job_location_selector`,
`job_department_selector`. However, `CareerPageFetcher` expects completely different key
names: `job_container`, `title`, `url`, `location`, `description`. The LLM-generated
strategies are stored in career_page_configs but the fetcher cannot extract any jobs
because it looks for keys that don't exist in the stored JSON.

## Affected Surfaces
- `pipeline/prompts/company_discovery.md:61-68` — scrape_strategy example with wrong keys (from drift review)
- `pipeline/prompts/company_discovery.md:91-98` — scrape_strategy field definitions with wrong keys (from drift review)
- `pipeline/src/fetchers/career_page.py:219` — expects `job_container` key (from drift review)
- `pipeline/src/fetchers/career_page.py:258-259` — expects `title`, `url` keys (from drift review)

## Fix
Update the prompt to use the fetcher's expected key names: `job_container`, `title`,
`url`, `location`, `description`. The fetcher's docstring (career_page.py:15-22) already
documents this format.

## Changes Needed
- `pipeline/prompts/company_discovery.md`: Replace `job_list_selector` with `job_container`, `job_title_selector` with `title`, `job_url_selector` with `url`, `job_location_selector` with `location`, `job_department_selector` with `description` in both the example and field definitions

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Prompt scrape_strategy example uses keys `job_container`, `title`, `url`, `location`, `description`
- [ ] Prompt scrape_strategy field definitions match fetcher-expected keys
- [ ] CareerPageFetcher can read LLM-generated scrape strategies without missing keys

title: Source filter ats_feed does not match DB platform-specific values
description:
## Root Cause
The job table's source filter dropdown contains `"ats_feed"` as a filter value, but the
normalizer writes platform-specific source names (`source="greenhouse"`, `source="lever"`,
`source="ashby"`) for ATS-sourced jobs. The value `"ats_feed"` is the `source_type`, not
the `source`. When a user selects "ats_feed", the query `j.source = 'ats_feed'` matches
zero rows.

## Affected Surfaces
- `web/components/job-table.tsx:42` — SOURCES array contains "ats_feed" (from drift review)
- `web/lib/queries.ts:200` — filter query uses `j.source = ?` (from drift review)

## Fix
Replace `"ats_feed"` in the SOURCES array with the individual ATS platform names:
`["adzuna", "remoteok", "linkedin", "career_page", "greenhouse", "lever", "ashby"]`.
Alternatively, change the filter to query `j.source_type` when "ats_feed" is selected.

## Changes Needed
- `web/components/job-table.tsx`: Replace `"ats_feed"` with `"greenhouse"`, `"lever"`, `"ashby"` in SOURCES array

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] SOURCES array contains platform-specific ATS names instead of "ats_feed"
- [ ] Filtering by any ATS platform returns matching jobs from the database
- [ ] All source filter values correspond to actual `source` column values in the DB

title: CareerPageFetcher omits company field from output dict
description:
## Root Cause
`CareerPageFetcher._parse_job_element` (career_page.py:264-279) returns dicts with keys
`title`, `url`, `_career_page_config_id`, `source`, and optionally `location` and
`description` — but never `company`. The `normalize_career_page` function
(normalizer.py:245) reads `raw.get("company", "Unknown")`, so every career-page job gets
`company="Unknown"` after normalization, losing the company association. By contrast,
`ATSFetcher` correctly injects `_company_name` into each raw dict.

## Affected Surfaces
- `pipeline/src/fetchers/career_page.py:264-279` — _parse_job_element output dict missing company (from drift review)
- `pipeline/src/normalizer.py:245` — defaults to "Unknown" for missing company (from drift review)

## Fix
Have CareerPageFetcher inject the company name into each raw dict, similar to ATSFetcher.
The fetcher already queries career_page_configs (which has company_id); extend the query
to JOIN companies and include `_company_name` in each output dict. Then update
normalize_career_page to read `_company_name`.

## Changes Needed
- `pipeline/src/fetchers/career_page.py`: JOIN companies table and inject `_company_name` into output dicts
- `pipeline/src/normalizer.py`: Update normalize_career_page to read `_company_name` (or `company`) from raw dict

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] CareerPageFetcher output dicts include company name field
- [ ] normalize_career_page reads company name from raw dict instead of defaulting to "Unknown"
- [ ] Career-page jobs retain correct company association after normalization

title: Column name crunchbase_data misleads for levels.fyi data
description:
## Root Cause
The `enrich()` function in levelsfy.py writes levels.fyi compensation data into a column
named `crunchbase_data` (levelsfy.py:130). A developer reading the companies table schema
would assume `crunchbase_data` contains Crunchbase API data exclusively. The column was
created for Crunchbase enrichment and later reused for levels.fyi data without renaming.

## Affected Surfaces
- `pipeline/src/enrichment/levelsfy.py:130` — writes to `crunchbase_data` (from clarity review)

## Fix
Rename the column to `enrichment_data` or `comp_data`, or add a dedicated `levelsfy_data`
column. If a schema migration is out of scope, add a prominent inline comment at
levelsfy.py:130 explaining why this column is reused and that the value is NOT Crunchbase data.

## Changes Needed
- `pipeline/src/enrichment/levelsfy.py`: Rename column or add explanatory comment

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Column name accurately reflects its contents, or a clear comment explains the reuse
- [ ] No developer confusion about what data the column stores

title: Bare except clause catches BaseException in adzuna fetcher
description:
## Root Cause
Inside `_fetch_page`, the inner exception handler at adzuna.py:189 uses a bare `except:`
clause. This catches all exceptions including `SystemExit`, `KeyboardInterrupt`, and
`GeneratorExit`. If the process receives a SIGINT during `e.response.json()`, the
`KeyboardInterrupt` is caught and re-raises `e` instead, masking the user's intent to
stop the program.

## Affected Surfaces
- `pipeline/src/fetchers/adzuna.py:189` — bare `except:` clause (from edge-cases review)

## Fix
Replace bare `except:` with `except (ValueError, AttributeError):` to catch only JSON
decode failures and missing `.response` attribute.

## Changes Needed
- `pipeline/src/fetchers/adzuna.py`: Replace `except:` with `except (ValueError, AttributeError):`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Exception handler catches only `ValueError` and `AttributeError`
- [ ] `KeyboardInterrupt` propagates normally through this code path
- [ ] JSON decode failures are still handled gracefully

title: Explicit None in nested dict access causes AttributeError
description:
## Root Cause
`normalize_adzuna` uses `raw.get("company", {}).get("display_name", "Unknown")`. The
default `{}` only protects against a missing `company` key. If the Adzuna API returns
`"company": null` (explicit JSON null), `raw.get("company", {})` returns `None` (not
`{}`), and the chained `.get("display_name")` raises `AttributeError: 'NoneType' object
has no attribute 'get'`. The same pattern appears for `location` on line 50.

## Affected Surfaces
- `pipeline/src/normalizer.py:46` — company field chained access (from edge-cases review)
- `pipeline/src/normalizer.py:50` — location field chained access (from edge-cases review)

## Fix
Use `(raw.get("company") or {}).get("display_name", "Unknown")` — the `or {}` coalesces
both missing and null values.

## Changes Needed
- `pipeline/src/normalizer.py`: Apply `or {}` pattern at lines 46 and 50

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `normalize_adzuna` handles explicit `null` for `company` without AttributeError
- [ ] `normalize_adzuna` handles explicit `null` for `location` without AttributeError
- [ ] Missing keys still use default values correctly

title: Empty LLM content list causes IndexError in company discovery
description:
## Root Cause
`_call_llm` in company_discovery.py accesses `message.content[0].text` without checking
that `message.content` is non-empty. If the Claude API returns a message with an empty
`content` array (which can happen on max_tokens=0 or certain error conditions), this
raises `IndexError`. The surrounding `except Exception` catches this but logs a
misleading "LLM call failed" message.

## Affected Surfaces
- `pipeline/src/company_discovery.py:250` — `content[0]` on possibly-empty list (from edge-cases review)

## Fix
Add `if not message.content: return None` before the indexing, or check
`len(message.content) > 0`.

## Changes Needed
- `pipeline/src/company_discovery.py`: Add empty-content guard before `message.content[0].text`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Empty `message.content` is handled gracefully without IndexError
- [ ] Log message accurately describes the failure cause

title: Large job batches exceed SQLite variable limit in deduplicator
description:
## Root Cause
The IN clause in `deduplicate_and_insert` creates one `?` placeholder per incoming job
URL. SQLite has a compile-time max of `SQLITE_MAX_VARIABLE_NUMBER` (default 999 in many
builds). If called with 1000+ jobs on a system with the 999 limit, this raises
`sqlite3.OperationalError: too many SQL variables`. The same pattern repeats at line 134
for hash lookup.

## Affected Surfaces
- `pipeline/src/deduplicator.py:103` — URL lookup IN clause (from edge-cases review)
- `pipeline/src/deduplicator.py:134` — hash lookup IN clause (from edge-cases review)

## Fix
Process the IN-clause query in chunks of 500 (well under any reasonable limit), or use a
temp table approach.

## Changes Needed
- `pipeline/src/deduplicator.py`: Chunk IN-clause queries into batches of 500 at lines 103 and 134

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] URL lookup works correctly with 1000+ job batches
- [ ] Hash lookup works correctly with 1000+ job batches
- [ ] No sqlite3.OperationalError for large batches

title: Missing DB file auto-creates empty database with no schema
description:
## Root Cause
`new Database(DB_PATH)` (better-sqlite3) creates the file if it doesn't exist rather than
failing. If the pipeline has not yet run and `data/jobs.db` is absent, the web app silently
creates an empty SQLite database. Every subsequent query throws `SqliteError: no such
table: jobs`.

## Affected Surfaces
- `web/lib/db.ts:32` — Database creation with no existence check (from edge-cases review)

## Fix
Before creating the Database instance, check that the file exists:
`if (!fs.existsSync(DB_PATH)) throw new Error('Database not found at ${DB_PATH}. Run the pipeline first.')`

## Changes Needed
- `web/lib/db.ts`: Add `fs.existsSync(DB_PATH)` check before `new Database(DB_PATH)`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Missing database file throws a clear error message instead of creating empty DB
- [ ] Error message mentions running the pipeline first
- [ ] Existing database file works normally

title: parseSkills assumes array but pipeline may store object format
description:
## Root Cause
The `parseSkills` function in skills-comparison.tsx parses `extracted_skills` as a JSON
array via `Array.isArray(parsed)`. However, the `resume_parser.md` prompt instructs the
LLM to output a JSON object with keys `skills`, `experience_timeline`, `accomplishments`,
`seniority_indicators`. If the pipeline stores this full object, `Array.isArray(parsed)`
returns false and the function returns `[]`, causing Skills Comparison to show 0 resume skills.

## Affected Surfaces
- `web/components/skills-comparison.tsx:18-29` — parseSkills assumes array format (from correctness review)

## Fix
Handle both array and object formats defensively: if parsed is an object with a `skills`
key, extract that array.

## Changes Needed
- `web/components/skills-comparison.tsx`: Update parseSkills to handle both array and `{skills: [...]}` object formats

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] parseSkills returns skills when input is a plain JSON array
- [ ] parseSkills returns skills when input is a JSON object with `skills` key
- [ ] parseSkills returns empty array for invalid/null input

title: Resume parser prompt references wrong column name content vs suggested_change
description:
## Root Cause
The resume_parser.md prompt (line 95) says "the pipeline records a suggestion with
suggestion_type = 'add_skill' and content set to the skill name." However, the actual DB
column is `suggested_change` (database.py:154, models.py:228). While this is purely
informational (the LLM doesn't generate SQL), it will mislead anyone reading the prompt
alongside the database schema.

## Affected Surfaces
- `pipeline/prompts/resume_parser.md:95` — references `content` instead of `suggested_change` (from drift review)

## Fix
Change "content set to the skill name" to "suggested_change set to the skill name".

## Changes Needed
- `pipeline/prompts/resume_parser.md`: Replace "content" with "suggested_change" at line 95

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Prompt text references `suggested_change` column correctly
- [ ] Column name in prompt matches actual DB schema

title: print() vs logger inconsistency in older pipeline modules
description:
## Root Cause
Older modules (adzuna, remoteok, filter_jobs) use `print()` for status output instead of
`logging.getLogger(__name__)`. All other fetchers (ats, career_page, linkedin) and most
pipeline modules use `logger` consistently. This means output from these modules cannot be
controlled via logging configuration.

## Affected Surfaces
- `pipeline/src/fetchers/adzuna.py:87-88,94,134,136,140-142` — print() calls (from clarity review)
- `pipeline/src/fetchers/remoteok.py:44,62,69,74` — print() calls (from clarity review)
- `pipeline/src/filter.py:229-249` — print() calls in filter_jobs (from clarity review)

## Fix
Replace all `print()` calls with `logger.info()` or `logger.debug()` to match the
convention established by newer modules.

## Changes Needed
- `pipeline/src/fetchers/adzuna.py`: Replace print() with logger calls
- `pipeline/src/fetchers/remoteok.py`: Replace print() with logger calls
- `pipeline/src/filter.py`: Replace print() with logger calls in filter_jobs()

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] No `print()` calls remain in adzuna.py, remoteok.py, or filter.py
- [ ] All status output uses `logger.info()` or `logger.debug()`
- [ ] Logger is initialized via `logging.getLogger(__name__)` in each module

title: British spelling normalise inconsistent with codebase
description:
## Root Cause
The function `normalise` at skills-comparison.tsx:60 uses British spelling. The rest of
the codebase uses American English exclusively (e.g., `normalize` in deduplicator.py:32).
A developer searching for "normalize" would not find this function.

## Affected Surfaces
- `web/components/skills-comparison.tsx:60` — function named `normalise` (from clarity review)

## Fix
Rename to `normalize` for consistency.

## Changes Needed
- `web/components/skills-comparison.tsx`: Rename `normalise` to `normalize`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Function is named `normalize` consistent with codebase convention

title: Emoji in adzuna print output inconsistent with codebase
description:
## Root Cause
Line 140 of adzuna.py outputs a warning emoji in a print statement. No other file in the
pipeline or web layer uses emojis in output, making this a minor style inconsistency.

## Affected Surfaces
- `pipeline/src/fetchers/adzuna.py:140` — emoji in print output (from clarity review)

## Fix
Replace the emoji with a plain text prefix like `WARNING:` or remove it entirely.

## Changes Needed
- `pipeline/src/fetchers/adzuna.py`: Remove emoji from output at line 140

## Acceptance Criteria

title: Naive datetime comparison when posted_at lacks timezone
description:
## Root Cause
`is_too_old` in filter.py replaces a `"Z"` suffix with `"+00:00"` for timezone-aware
parsing, but if `posted_at` is a date-only string like `"2026-03-01"` (no "Z", no
timezone), `fromisoformat` produces a naive datetime. The subsequent
`datetime.now(posted_date.tzinfo)` then calls `datetime.now(None)`, which returns local
system time. This mixes UTC-origin data with local-time "now", leading to incorrect age
calculation by up to +/-12 hours.

## Affected Surfaces
- `pipeline/src/filter.py:121` — timezone-naive datetime comparison (from edge-cases review)

## Fix
Normalize to UTC: `if posted_date.tzinfo is None: posted_date = posted_date.replace(tzinfo=timezone.utc)`.

## Changes Needed
- `pipeline/src/filter.py`: Add UTC normalization for timezone-naive posted_date

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Date-only strings are treated as UTC
- [ ] Age calculation is correct regardless of server timezone

title: Truthiness check rejects valid zero in server actions
description:
## Root Cause
`submitFeedback` checks `if (!jobId || ...)` where `jobId = Number(formData.get("job_id"))`.
If `job_id` is `"0"`, `Number("0")` is `0`, and `!0` is `true`, so the validation rejects
it. AUTOINCREMENT IDs start at 1 so `id=0` never occurs in practice, but the pattern is
semantically incorrect. The same pattern appears in `updateCompanyCareerUrl` (line 75) and
`toggleCompanyTarget` (line 98).

## Affected Surfaces
- `web/app/actions.ts:27` — submitFeedback truthiness check (from edge-cases review)
- `web/app/actions.ts:75` — updateCompanyCareerUrl truthiness check (from edge-cases review)
- `web/app/actions.ts:98` — toggleCompanyTarget truthiness check (from edge-cases review)

## Fix
Use `isNaN(jobId)` instead of `!jobId` to distinguish invalid input from zero.

## Changes Needed
- `web/app/actions.ts`: Replace `!jobId` with `isNaN(jobId)` at lines 27, 75, 98

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Validation correctly rejects NaN input
- [ ] Validation does not reject valid numeric zero (even if theoretical)

title: Enrichment enrich() signatures omit company_id parameter
description:
## Root Cause
The seek-10 task specifies `enrich(company_id, company_name, db_connection) -> bool` but
all four enrichment modules implement `enrich(company_name, db_connection)` without
`company_id`, looking up companies via `WHERE name = ?` instead of by ID. This works
correctly due to the UNIQUE constraint on `name` but deviates from the documented interface.

## Affected Surfaces
- `pipeline/src/enrichment/crunchbase.py` — missing company_id param (from correctness review)
- `pipeline/src/enrichment/glassdoor.py` — missing company_id param (from correctness review)
- `pipeline/src/enrichment/levelsfy.py` — missing company_id param (from correctness review)
- `pipeline/src/enrichment/stackshare.py` — missing company_id param (from correctness review)

## Fix
Add `company_id` as an optional parameter, or document the name-based lookup as the
intended interface.

## Changes Needed
- `pipeline/src/enrichment/crunchbase.py`, `glassdoor.py`, `levelsfy.py`, `stackshare.py`: Add optional company_id parameter or update documentation

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Enrichment interface matches documented specification, or documentation is updated to match implementation

title: None becomes string None in filter f-string interpolation
description:
## Root Cause
`should_filter` builds `text = f"{job.title} {job.description}".lower()`. When
`job.description` is `None`, Python's f-string converts it to the literal string `"None"`,
so the search text becomes e.g. `"senior data engineer none"`. While unlikely to match
real red flag keywords, this is semantically incorrect.

## Affected Surfaces
- `pipeline/src/filter.py:139` — None becomes "None" in f-string (from edge-cases review)

## Fix
Use `f"{job.title or ''} {job.description or ''}"`.

## Changes Needed
- `pipeline/src/filter.py`: Apply `or ''` pattern at line 139

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] None description does not produce literal "None" in search text
- [ ] Filter still works correctly with valid description strings

title: INNER JOIN in career_page configs silently drops orphaned rows
description:
## Root Cause
The seek-38 fix changed `_query_active_configs` from a standalone SELECT on
`career_page_configs` to a `JOIN companies c ON c.id = cpc.company_id` (INNER JOIN).
If a `career_page_configs` row has a `company_id` not present in the `companies` table
(e.g., company manually deleted or data integrity gap), the INNER JOIN silently drops
that config. The config would never be crawled and no warning is emitted. Foreign key
integrity is enforced by the pipeline, so orphaned rows are unlikely — but the failure
mode is silent, making it hard to diagnose if it does occur.

## Affected Surfaces
- `pipeline/src/fetchers/career_page.py:160` — INNER JOIN may exclude orphaned configs (from edge-cases review)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Orphaned career_page_configs rows are either included via LEFT JOIN or logged as warnings
